conflict: Everyone now wants proof, but proof requires permits, equipment, and institutional trust Mina no longer has
consequence: The eel hunt becomes a camp-level opportunity and liability rather than Mina's private obsession.
must_land_beats: Mina attempts permits and chain-of-custody procedures and is repeatedly forced to call people who remember her disgrace.
Imogen recognizes the footage could save the camp and starts treating the eel as an asset.
Tomas compounds his audit risk by borrowing better gear through county channels.
